title: Restructure agent prompt layout for cache-optimized shared prefix across 13 agents
description: MindPattern dispatches 13 research agents in parallel via claude -p. Each agent's prompt is built by build_agent_prompt() with this layout: JSON schema → soul.md → user.md → agent skill → dynamic context → novelty requirement → research methodology → phases → schema repeated. The problem: agent-specific content (skill file) is sandwiched between shared static content (soul.md/user.md above, novelty/methodology below). This prevents Claude's automatic prompt caching from working because the shared prefix diverges at position 4 (skill file). Restructure to maximize the shared prefix: JSON schema → soul.md → user.md → novelty requirement → research methodology → schema bottom → THEN agent-specific content (skill file, dynamic context, trends, claims, preflight items). The first ~70% of every prompt becomes identical across all 13 agents, enabling prompt caching. Research from Mavik Labs shows prompt caching achieves 45-80% cost reduction and 13-31% faster time-to-first-token. With 13 parallel agents sharing a common prefix, the savings compound significantly.
requirements: Restructure build_agent_prompt() to place all shared static content as a contiguous prefix
Shared prefix order: JSON output schema top → soul.md → user.md → novelty requirement → research methodology → self-critique gate → JSON output schema bottom
Agent-specific suffix order: agent skill file → dynamic context (date, trends, claims) → Phase 1 preflight items → Phase 2 explore instructions
Verify no semantic change to agent behavior — the same information is present, just reordered
Add a comment in build_agent_prompt() documenting the cache-optimized layout rationale
Measure prompt prefix length vs total prompt length and log the cache-eligible ratio
done_criteria: All 13 agents receive prompts with identical first N characters (where N > 50% of total prompt length)
Agent output quality does not regress (compare finding counts and quality from one run before vs after)
No changes to agent dispatch, parsing, or downstream pipeline phases

design_spec.architecture_impact: Pure prompt restructuring inside build_agent_prompt(). No changes to run_single_agent(), dispatch_research_agents(), or any other function. The Claude CLI handles prompt caching automatically when it detects shared prefixes across concurrent requests within a time window.

context: LLM cost optimization research shows prompt caching achieves 45-80% cost reduction when concurrent requests share a common prefix. MindPattern runs 13 agents in parallel with ~70% shared content (schema, soul.md, user.md, methodology) but currently interleaves agent-specific content in the middle, breaking cache eligibility. Source: https://www.maviklabs.com/blog/llm-cost-optimization-2026
